reasoning: The base rate for the US launching a ground invasion or seizing territory of a heavily armed nation like Iran is extremely low. While the US did conduct an air campaign against Iranian nuclear facilities in late June 2025, numerous credible sources immediately clarified that there was no intent to deploy ground troops or invade the country. By August 2025, reports indicated that Iran had approved the resumption of nuclear negotiations with the US, pointing strongly to diplomatic de-escalation rather than further conflict. As of the November 9, 2025 cutoff, there are no unclassified reports or indications of US forces preparing to establish control over any portion of Iranian territory. With less than two months remaining in the year, the logistical and political hurdles make an untelegraphed invasion extremely unlikely.
